title: Salesforce API Access Volume Anomalies
dataSources: `index=salesforce` (align with your TA output index) fed by the Splunk Add-on for Salesforce: **`salesforce:event_log`** is built from Salesforce **Event Log File (ELF)** records retrieved via the **Event Log File REST API**, typically **polled hourly (3600s)** by the modular input—**requires Salesforce Event Monitoring add-on and/or Salesforce Shield** licensing because ELF APIs are entitlement-gated. For this UC, filter on **`EventType="ApiTotalUsage"`**; typical auto-extracted fields include **`UserId`**, **`ClientId`**, **`ConnectedApp`**, **`EventType`**, hour-bucketed API counters, and timestamps (verify against your TA version’s `props.conf`/`fields.conf` because Salesforce occasionally renames or adds attributes). **`salesforce:login_history`** is collected via the **Login History REST API** (separate input stanza) and supplies session context (login time, source IP/ASN where exposed, application) to correlate human vs. integration traffic with API bursts. **Data flow:** Salesforce cloud → OAuth-authenticated REST pull on Heavy Forwarder or Search Head collection tier → parsed JSON → `index=salesforce`. **CIM note:** **No standard Splunk CIM mapping** exists for Salesforce Event Monitoring; treat events as **proprietary Salesforce JSON** and enrich with lookups (integration catalog, service principals) rather than expecting accelerated CIM `tstats`.
app: `Splunk Add-on for Salesforce` ([Splunkbase 3549](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3549)), ≥5.0. Install on the Search Head (for field extractions and saved searches) and on a dedicated Heavy Forwarder if running data collection off-SH. The TA provides both collection and parsing. The TA requires a Salesforce Connected App with OAuth 2.0 credentials and an Event Monitoring or Salesforce Shield license.
spl:
index=salesforce sourcetype="salesforce:login_history" OR sourcetype="salesforce:event_log" EventType="ApiTotalUsage"
| stats count as api_calls by UserId, ClientId, ConnectedApp
| eventstats avg(api_calls) as avg_calls, stdev(api_calls) as sd by ConnectedApp
| where api_calls > avg_calls + 3*sd
| sort -api_calls
description: Detects unusual spikes in API call volumes per user, OAuth client, and connected application by comparing each tuple to per-app mean and standard deviation—signaling possible credential abuse, data scraping, or compromised integrations.
value: Runaway programmatic access can exfiltrate customer and transaction data, driving breach costs, regulatory penalties, and fraud losses; early detection limits account takeover impact and supports sound access governance for auditors and customers.
implementation: Ingest Salesforce Event Monitoring and login-related feeds into a dedicated index; baseline API totals per connected app; tune the 3-sigma multiplier and minimum event volume for your tenant’s integration mix.
visualization: **Row 1 —** Single value + bar chart: count of `(UserId,ClientId,ConnectedApp)` tuples exceeding 3σ in the last 24h (default time picker **Last 24 hours**); bar chart top-15 `ConnectedApp` by max `api_calls` with drilldown to raw `ApiTotalUsage` events. **Row 2 —** Timechart (line, `span=1h`) of total `api_calls` by `ConnectedApp` with overlay of alert threshold markers; drilldown sets token `$app$` to filter the row-3 table. **Row 3 —** Sortable table: `UserId`, `ClientId`, `ConnectedApp`, `api_calls`, cohort `avg_calls`, `sd`, z-score; cell highlighting when `api_calls > avg_calls+3*sd`. **Row 4 —** Linked **Login History** panel (`salesforce:login_history`) filtered by `UserId` from table click-through, showing `Application`, `Browser`, `Platform`, and source IP if present for session tie-in.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable for the primary search: it relies on application- and industry-specific log formats, not a normalized CIM data model.

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Salesforce — Data Collection Configuration

knownFalsePositives: (a) **Month-end financial close automation:** Salesforce CPQ, billing, and ERP syncs legitimately spike `ApiTotalUsage` for integration users—**mitigate** by correlating spikes with `index=*` change tickets, ServiceNow change windows, or a `salesforce_integration_accounts` lookup keyed on `ConnectedApp`/`ClientId` to suppress known batch principals while still logging to a summary index. (b) **Data-lake and warehouse ETL:** Informatica, MuleSoft, Fivetran, or internal Python jobs run wide SOQL/Bulk patterns hourly—**mitigate** with per-app baselines (raise sigma for stable apps), `where sd>0`, and minimum-event floors so low-variance tenants do not page. (c) **Marketing automation bursts:** Marketo/Salesforce connectors can hammer APIs during list syncs—**mitigate** by tagging marketing service accounts in IAM and joining campaign calendars. (d) **Sandbox refresh and full-copy activities:** Refreshes replay metadata and drive atypical API totals—**mitigate** by routing production-only indexes in the alert SPL or maintaining `sf_org_id` production allowlists. (e) **New integration go-lives:** First-week traffic looks like an outlier until the baseline window includes post-cutover days—**mitigate** with a 14–30 day warmup period and dynamic thresholds per `ConnectedApp`. (f) **Penetration tests and red-team API abuse simulations:** Deliberately high volumes mimic compromise—**mitigate** with `pentest_windows` lookups and SecOps channel pre-registration so tests do not trigger production paging.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• **`Splunk Add-on for Salesforce` ≥5.0** deployed on **Search Heads** (knowledge objects, scheduled searches, dashboard drilldowns) and, for production-grade collection isolation, on a **dedicated Heavy Forwarder** acting as the OAuth client to Salesforce.
• **Salesforce entitlements:** access to **Event Log File (ELF) REST APIs** requires **Salesforce Event Monitoring add-on** and/or relevant **Shield** capabilities—confirm in your Salesforce SKUs before building detections.
• **Connected App:** create a Salesforce Connected App enabling **OAuth 2.0** (client credentials or JWT bearer flow consistent with Splunk TA documentation); least-privilege integration user; store secrets in Splunk credential storage—not `local/inputs.conf` in clear text.
• **Indexing & RBAC:** route TA output to **`index=salesforce`** (or prod-only sibling); grant SOC and IAM analyst roles **`srchIndexesAllowed`** without admin; optionally separate `_internal` access for HF troubleshooting.
• **Lookup inventory:** maintain **`salesforce_integration_accounts`** (`ClientId`, `ConnectedApp`, `owner`, `change_id`, `suppress`) and optional **`pentest_windows`** for coordinated tests.
• **License & volume planning:** ELF polling defaults to **3600s**; total ingest scales with active users, API traffic, and enabled EventTypes—pilot 7 days and extrapolate GB/day before promoting alerts.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable both **`salesforce:event_log`** and **`salesforce:login_history`** modular inputs per [Splunk Add-on for Salesforce — Data Collection Configuration](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/AddOns/released/Salesforce/Configureinputs). For ELF, set the interval to **3600** seconds unless Salesforce API guidance for your edition permits a shorter poll (shorter polls increase API quota use and Splunk license). Verify the TA ingests the **`ApiTotalUsage`** EventType by running:

```spl
index=salesforce sourcetype="salesforce:event_log" EventType="ApiTotalUsage" earliest=-24h
| stats count by EventType, ConnectedApp
```

If counts are zero, confirm **Event Monitoring** entitlements, integration user permissions, and that the TA’s **Event Log file type** selection includes API usage categories for your org.

Example **Heavy Forwarder** fragment (illustrative—adapt names to TA version):

```ini
[salesforce://<instance_name>/event_log]
index = salesforce
interval = 3600

[salesforce://<instance_name>/login_history]
index = salesforce
interval = 3600
```

On the **Search Head**, confirm field extractions align with JSON paths for **`UserId`**, **`ClientId`**, **`ConnectedApp`**, and counters; use **Field Extractor** or `| fieldsummary` after sampling 10k events.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
```spl
index=salesforce sourcetype="salesforce:login_history" OR sourcetype="salesforce:event_log" EventType="ApiTotalUsage"
| stats count as api_calls by UserId, ClientId, ConnectedApp
| eventstats avg(api_calls) as avg_calls, stdev(api_calls) as sd by ConnectedApp
| where sd>0 AND api_calls > avg_calls + 3*sd
| sort - api_calls
```

Understanding this SPL
• The base search intentionally unions **`salesforce:event_log`** (where `ApiTotalUsage` lives) with **`salesforce:login_history`** as a guardrail if your mapping accidentally routes some API-hour summaries through login-oriented sourcetypes during TA upgrades—tighten to `sourcetype="salesforce:event_log"` if duplicates inflate counts.
• **`stats ... by UserId, ClientId, ConnectedApp`** forms the **unit of risk**: a human or integration principal, OAuth client, and app registration tuple.
• **`eventstats ... by ConnectedApp`** computes **cohort statistics inside the result set** without losing per-row detail—each row compares to its own app’s mean and spread.
• **`where sd>0`** suppresses divide-by-zero and **low-variance noise** where standard deviation is undefined or trivial after a quiet hour.
• **`api_calls > avg_calls + 3*sd`** is a classic **3-sigma** guardrail; move to **4–5σ** for chatty marketing apps, or swap to **median absolute deviation** for heavy-tailed integrations if math reviews require robust statistics.
• Consider adding **`| lookup salesforce_integration_accounts ClientId OUTPUT suppress`** and **`| where suppress!=1`** to operationalize allowlists without editing SPL weekly.

Schedule as Alert: cron **`5 * * * *`** (five minutes after the hour to let ELF files finalize), time range **`-65m@m` to `-5m@m`**, trigger **number of results > 0**, **throttle** on **`ConnectedApp` + `UserId`** for **4 hours**, priority **P3** unless paired with impossible-travel (UC-10.12.49) for same `UserId`.

Step 3 — Validate
(a) **Existence check:** `index=salesforce EventType="ApiTotalUsage" earliest=-7d | timechart span=1d count` should be non-zero for production orgs.
(b) **Field sanity:** `| fieldsummary UserId ClientId ConnectedApp`—null rates above 5% indicate parsing drift.
(c) **Cohort stability:** run the alert SPL in **stats mode** over 30 days with `| where false` replaced by `| stats count by ConnectedApp` to ensure each app has **≥30 hourly buckets** before enforcement.
(d) **Ground truth:** pick one spike, compare **`ConnectedApp`** to **Setup → App Manager → Connected Apps** and API Usage screens in Salesforce.
(e) **Cross-log:** join `UserId` to **`salesforce:login_history`** in the same window to see if sessions originate from **new ASN/country**.
(f) **Role permissions:** `| rest /services/authentication/current-context` confirms the service account executing the scheduled search retains index rights.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard layout: mirrors **Visualization** section—**Row 1** KPI + top apps bar; **Row 2** hourly trendlines; **Row 3** analyst table with z-score; **Row 4** login corroboration. **Alerting:** send **Splunk webhook → PagerDuty** with `{UserId, ClientId, ConnectedApp, api_calls, avg_calls, sd}` JSON payload; mirror to **Slack #crm-security** informational. **Runbook (SOC):** (1) confirm not a **`pentest_window`** entry; (2) query IAM for **`UserId`** employment state; (3) revoke **`ClientId`** refresh tokens via admin runbook if compromise suspected; (4) open Salesforce **Login History**/`ApiTotalUsage` detail for the hour; (5) attach evidence to insider-risk or incident ticket.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Symptom — Zero `ApiTotalUsage` events.** Root cause: missing Event Monitoring entitlement, paused input, wrong org URL, or TA credentials expired. Fix: validate Connected App oauth token refresh in **`splunkd.log`** on collector; re-authorize TA; rerun probe search.
• **Symptom — Alert floods at every hour boundary.** Root cause: **ELF poll batches** concentrating events at ingestion time; baseline uses too-short window. Fix: widen lookback to 24–72h median for `avg_calls`; use **median** instead of mean for skewed counts.
• **Symptom — Massive duplicates doubling `api_calls`.** Root cause: overlapping inputs (prod vs sandbox) indexing same payloads. Fix: constrain `sf_org_id` with `WHERE`/`search` macros; segregate indexes.
• **Symptom — Legitimate integrations always fire.** Root cause: 3σ too aggressive for always-on ETL. Fix: per-app sigma overrides via **`lookup sf_api_sigma_override ConnectedApp OUTPUT sigma`** replacing literal `3`.
• **Symptom — `sd` always null.** Root cause: only one tuple per `ConnectedApp` in window. Fix: increase schedule window to multi-hour or switch to **global** app baselines using **summary indexing**.
• **Symptom — Analyst table shows GUIDs only.** Root cause: missing HR/SCIM join. Fix: `lookup salesforce_users UserId OUTPUT Username Email Division`.
• **Symptom — Parsing errors post-upgrade.** Root cause: TA upgrade altered JSON paths. Fix: reinstall TA Sideview on HF/SH bundle; replay sample events through `btool props list` audit.

Extended baseline engineering for API volume anomalies
Financial institutions typically operate dozens of **connected applications**—CPQ, KYC, data warehouses, and mobile gateways—each with different hour-of-day shapes. Where the core alert uses **per-ConnectedApp mean and standard deviation**, mature programs add **day-type stratification**: compute baselines separately for **business days**, **month-end**, and **known marketing campaign days** using a `business_calendar` lookup that feeds `eval day_type=if(...)` before `eventstats`. This prevents chronic false negatives when month-end batch windows legitimately shift the distribution. For multinational firms, also stratify by **global region** when user populations are separated, either by normalizing `UserId` with a region attribute from your HR feed or by splitting on `ClientId` patterns that map to regional gateways.

Summary indexing pattern (optional but recommended)
Materialize hourly aggregates into a summary index (for example, `summary_sf_api`) with a populating search such as: `index=salesforce sourcetype="salesforce:event_log" EventType="ApiTotalUsage" | bin _time span=1h | stats sum(<counter field>) as api_calls by _time, UserId, ClientId, ConnectedApp`. Choose the correct API counter field name for your TA version (validate with `fieldsummary`). The detection search then compares the **last completed hour** against trailing N days using `append` or `join` techniques, dramatically reducing raw scan costs. Keep the summary search on a **15–30 minute** schedule offset from ELF polling to avoid partial hours.

Identity corroboration with IdP and HR signals
When a tuple indicates a **human** rather than a service principal, enrich with **IdP** authentication risk (`Okta System Log`, `Azure AD sign-ins`, or `Ping`) in a **second-stage** search template stored as a macro `sf_api_correlate_idp( user )`. Common corroboration patterns include: **new device fingerprint**, **impossible travel** aligned in time with the API spike, **MFA fatigue** or **MFA bypass** flags, and **terminated employee** status from HRIS feeds. For **service principals**, corroborate with **CI/CD** deployment logs and **change tickets**. Document that API telemetry alone is **insufficient** for hostile attribution; it is a **high-quality triage signal**.

Robust statistics beyond 3σ
Gaussian assumptions often fail for API workloads. Two audit-friendly alternatives: (1) **Median Absolute Deviation**—replace `avg`/`stdev` with `median`/`mad` approximations using `eventstats median(api_calls) as med | eventstats abs(api_calls-med) as dev | eventstats median(dev) as mad` (with care for performance), flagging when `api_calls > med + k*mad`. (2) **Quantile breach**—compare current hour to trailing 30-day `perc95`/`perc99` via summary data. Maintain parallel searches during migration and retire the legacy logic only after **operational sign-off**.

Alerting nuance: throttles, severities, and paging ethics
Use **two-tier routing**: Tier-1 routes **integration-only** tuples to an integration SRE queue with **email** priority; Tier-2 routes **human user** tuples with **PagerDuty** if correlated risk exceeds two independent signals (for example, API spike + new country login). **Throttle** using a compound key `UserId + ClientId + ConnectedApp` rather than `UserId` alone to avoid missing account hopping across apps. Document **quiet hours** policies humanely—financial close may be stressful enough without redundant pages; use Slack for advisory notices during known windows.

Validation experiments and purple teaming
Run annual **tabletop** exercises where application security injects a controlled traffic pattern through a test Connected App. Success criteria: detection within **two ELF poll cycles**, ticket created with correct fields, and mean time to contain under an agreed SLA. Capture **splunkdiag** excerpts and TA configuration snapshots as evidence. For purple team collaborations, replay captured JSON (**sanitized**) into a dev index to tune field aliasing without touching production OAuth scopes.

Operational analytics for service owners
Publish a weekly **heatmap** by `ConnectedApp` showing coefficient of variation; apps with exploding variance are **`drift candidates`** deserving proactive baseline refresh. Invite application owners to **subscribe** to their slice of results using Splunk **report acceleration** or email subscriptions of **zero-result** confirmations (silent success), which reinforces partnership between security and engineering.

Disaster recovery and collector failover
If the Heavy Forwarder performing OAuth loses network path to Salesforce, events gap. Maintain a **collector health** alert on `missing_sourcetypes` patterns and failover DNS or VIP to a standby HF with cloned credentials—testing quarterly. When catching up after outage, widen alert windows temporarily and use **historical replay** caution to avoid alerting on ingestion catch-up bursts; alternatively, pause alerting until **backfill completeness** KPIs satisfy SRE.

SPL hygiene and field drift management
Lock critical extractions in **props/transforms** under a custom app `TA_salesforce_local` so TA upgrades do not silently revert behavior. After upgrades, run `| fieldsummary` comparisons saved to a Git-managed CSV. If Salesforce changes JSON nesting, update **FIELDALIAS** or **JSON extraction** stanzas rather than modifying vendor defaults in place.

Insider threat program alignment
Coordinate with **insider risk** teams: API misuse may be a leading indicator of **pre-resignation data collection**. Ensure legal approves monitoring scope and that **union** or **works council** obligations are observed in applicable jurisdictions. Where required, **pseudonymize** certain display fields in lower environments.

Quarterly KPI set
Track: alert volume trend, suppression rate (%), MTTD, MTTR, % tickets with RCA, recurrence rate per `ConnectedApp`, and cost per million events scanned. Discuss anomalies in a **CRM security forum** chaired by IAM.

Governance, evidence, and continuous control testing
Establish this detection as a named IT General Control for custodial CRM data. Each quarter, export a statistically significant sample of alerts (for example, 25 cases) and perform a **quality review** covering five dimensions: (1) timeliness relative to Salesforce poll latency, (2) accuracy versus ground truth in Salesforce admin screens, (3) completeness of ticketing metadata, (4) appropriateness of suppressions, and (5) analyst narrative quality suitable for regulatory interviews. Store evidence in your GRC repository with hashes and retention aligned to your legal hold policy. When Splunk or the Salesforce TA upgrades, repeat a **regression harness** that replays frozen JSON fixtures through a dev indexer to ensure field extractions remain stable. Maintain a **change advisory** record whenever thresholds, lookup schemas, or cron schedules change, because auditors will ask for a traceable history of tuning decisions.

Performance, workload, and search optimization
Salesforce security searches can become expensive if they scan wide time ranges without partition discipline. Prefer **index-time routing** that isolates production org data, and use **`tstats` only when you have a compatible data model**—which is rarely true for raw Salesforce JSON—so focus on **fast filters** (`sourcetype`, `EventType`, `index`) and **summary indexing** for baselines. On Splunk Cloud, monitor **search budget** consumption for the service account running these schedules; split heavy baseline jobs from alert jobs. For on-premises, place collection on a **Heavy Forwarder** to keep REST polling off search heads. If you enable **workload management**, assign these searches to a pool with guardrails so ad-hoc human searches do not starve operational detections.

Risk communication and executive narrative
Translate technical hits into business language. For each incident class, maintain a one-paragraph **executive summary template** describing customer impact, financial exposure, and remediation status. This elevates the UC from a SOC tool to a board-ready control narrative, especially in financial services where CRM data includes wealth, insurance, or mortgage artifacts. Pair metrics with **leading indicators** (integration onboarding rate, active connected apps) so risk committees can ask forward-looking questions rather than only reviewing rear-view alert counts.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for unusual bursts of automated requests linked to each person’s login in our customer database system, because that pattern can mean a stolen password, a rogue program scraping records, or a broken integration behaving like an attacker.
